Sample Test Page

Home About Contact

About This Page

This is a sample HTML page for testing the gosearch web crawler and indexer.

The page contains various elements including headings, paragraphs, links, and lists.

Features

  • Web crawling with Colly
  • Inverted index construction
  • Boolean search queries
  • Fuzzy matching with Levenshtein distance
  • TF-IDF ranking
  • PageRank algorithm

Search Engine Components

Crawler
Traverses web pages and extracts content and links
Indexer
Builds an inverted index from crawled content
Ranker
Calculates relevance scores using TF-IDF and PageRank
Searcher
Processes queries and returns ranked results

Programming Languages

Modern search engines are implemented in various programming languages:

  • Go - Excellent for concurrent web scraping
  • Python - Great for prototyping and machine learning
  • Java - Enterprise-scale search platforms
  • C++ - High performance requirements
  • Rust - Memory safety with zero-cost abstractions

Boolean Query Examples

You can use boolean operators to refine your search:

  • search AND engine - Results containing both terms
  • crawler OR indexer - Results containing either term
  • web NOT spider - Results containing "web" but not "spider"
  • "inverted index" - Exact phrase match

Go Programming

Go is an excellent choice for building search engines:

  • Built-in concurrency with goroutines and channels
  • Fast compilation and execution
  • Strong type system and memory safety
  • Excellent standard library
  • Great ecosystem for web scraping (Colly)

External Links

  • Go Programming Language
  • Colly Web Scraping Framework
  • Redis - Caching Layer

© 2024 gosearch test page. This is a sample for testing purposes only.
